route: ROUTE 2 -- TWIST ANGULAR DECORRELATION (exclusion, not counting)
target: bound return count A(q,r) on r in [1,10], uniform in level k, via: passes come from <=3 adjacent birth levels (deep-tail) AND adjacent-level passes are angularly separated by the twist arccos(-1/6)=99.594deg, so near-parallel crowding is impossible.
verdict: REFUTED as an independent exclusion force -- STUCK. The twist does NOT decorrelate adjacent-scale arc directions; the angular-gap premise is empirically and structurally false.

geometry_of_the_twist.x_axis: e1 is an eigenvector, eigenvalue 3, PURE DILATION -- NOT rotated. angle(M e1, e1)=0.
geometry_of_the_twist.yz_plane: block [[0,-3],[3,-1]], trace -1, det 9, modulus 3, rotation cos t = -1/2/3 = -1/6, t=99.594deg. Only the yz-components rotate.
geometry_of_the_twist.consequence: the twist is a rotation of ONLY the yz-part of a direction; any x-aligned component is twist-invariant across all levels.

empirical_refutation_L6_L7.max_passes: L6 r10 = 5, L7 r10 = 4 (consistent with measured A0<=5) -- but NOT produced by angular separation.

adjacent_level_pass_angle_min.note: NOT bounded away from 0; the predicted definite angle gap is absent.

returns_are_same_level_dominated.note: the return count is overwhelmingly WITHIN one birth level; the twist rotates only BETWEEN levels, so it cannot touch the dominant term.

same_level_near_parallel_events.note: two DISTINCT fresh-level passes through one Cheb-10 ball, near-parallel. Triple-free forbids exact collinearity, NOT near-parallel. The twist gives no protection here (same level).

x_alignment_of_passes.note: arc directions carry a large x-component on average; the x-axis is exactly the twist's non-rotating eigendirection, so a substantial part of every direction escapes decorrelation.

structural_reason_it_fails.isotropy_kills_rotation: Each birth level already carries a near-ISOTROPIC spread of arc directions (the 124-step menu spans all directions; realized same-level pass angles span 4deg..90deg). A rotation maps an isotropic ensemble into itself, so it creates NO angular gap.
structural_reason_it_fails.menu_self_overlap_under_M: For EVERY menu direction d, min_j angle(M.d, d_j) over the menu is <= 9.462deg, and for many it is exactly 0. So rotating level-j's direction set by the twist to obtain level-(j+1)'s set reproduces essentially the SAME set of directions -- level-j and level-(j+1) arcs can always be near-parallel.
structural_reason_it_fails.summary: Angular decorrelation between two rotation-invariant ensembles is vacuous. The 'unspooling fishing line cannot drift back near-parallel' intuition is FALSE for this walk: adjacent scales offer a full spread of directions, so near-parallel returns are geometrically available at every scale, and same-level returns (the dominant term) are untouched by any inter-level rotation.

what_survives.deep_tail_still_holds: passes come from <=3 adjacent birth levels (j=0,1,2); far-level pairs essentially absent (0 at r=5, 2 at L7 r=10). This is the ONLY real exclusion and it is the previously-proven min|M^3 v|inf=24 packing fact, not an angular one.
what_survives.A0_le_5_measured: still true and level-stable L6/L7, consistent with route1/route4 -- but the bound is NOT explained by the twist.

reduction_is_circular_for_this_route: Route 2's angular force cannot bound the same-birth-level return count, which is exactly the SINGLE-LEVEL REFILL / ANTI-STACKING count b_m(r) that routes 1/3/4 already isolate as the sole residue. Route 2 provides no independent purchase on it; the honest residue is unchanged.
scripts: design/exclusion/route2_angular.py  (birth-tagged pass directions in dense Cheb-r balls, L6/L7; adjacent- vs same-level angle histograms, x-alignment, near-parallel stacking events)
inline menu-isotropy test: min_j angle(M.d_i, d_j) <= 9.462deg for all 124 menu directions (no rotation gap)
status: STUCK (route refuted); overall programme remains CLOSES-MODULO-CLEAN-LEMMA via the single-level refill linearity that routes 1/3/4 isolate.
